summary: Tested global search feature (open, search, navigate, close) and click/navigation regression (dashboard summary card + sidebar nav). All flows pass. Backend /api/search returns correct accent-insensitive grouped results. Frontend clicks fire as expected.

backend: GET /api/search?q=goiko -> 4 results (2 players Goikoetxea, 1 family, 1 payment)
GET /api/search?q=benjamin -> 4 results (accent-insensitive match for Benjamín A team + match + players)
frontend: Dashboard summary card click (summary-active) -> navigates to /jugadores?estado=activo (PASS - clicks fire normally, no regression)
open-global-search button click -> dialog opens, input focused (PASS)
Click first search result -> dialog closes + navigates to /jugadores (PASS)
Search 'antiguoko' -> match result -> click navigates to /partidos (PASS)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1165 lines and growing; consider splitting routes by domain (players/teams/matches/search) into separate routers for maintainability.
GlobalSearch.jsx: debounce timer is correct but no cleanup on unmount of pending timeout; minor — could leak a setState on unmount.
Global search has no minimum-length guard on the frontend (sends request for any single char). The backend already accepts len>=1; consider client-side debounce-only-after-2-chars to reduce noise.
Layout.jsx Ctrl/Cmd+K handler does not stop propagation; works in tests but may conflict with future shortcuts.
Search route uses GET /api/search with no rate limiting/size cap beyond 40 results — acceptable for current dataset.
seed_data_creation: Re-seeded demo data via POST /api/seed-demo before tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Click handlers in the app work fine via real browser automation (Playwright). The previous agent's screenshot-tool click failures were a tool-side issue, NOT a React regression. Global search (button + Cmd/Ctrl+K + overlay + search + navigate + close via Esc/X/backdrop) is fully functional. Backend /api/search supports accent-insensitive matching across players/teams/matches/families/inscriptions/payments.
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found. Earlier reported 'clicks not firing' was a screenshot-tool simulation limitation, not an app bug.
